Coco is more than just an animated film — it’s an emotional journey that hit me straight in the heart. The movie beautifully blends music, family, and tradition, but what made it unforgettable for me was how deeply it captured the feeling of loneliness and being forgotten through Héctor’s story. I truly felt his pain — the years of being misunderstood, unloved, and left behind, waiting for someone to finally remember him. His suffering was powerful and real, and when Miguel discovers the truth and their bond as family, it became one of the most emotional moments I’ve ever seen in a movie. What I also loved was Miguel’s realization: that music is not just about dreams, but about connection, love, and remembering those who came before us. Watching the Rivera family come together in the end felt healing and complete. This movie deserves every bit of a 10/10 because it’s not just entertainment — it’s a reminder about love, memory, and never giving up, even in the face of being forgotten.

20th Century Studios had the Book of Life, Pixar had this. A wonderful animated Day of the Dead movie in which a boy of music making dreams which a long lost ancestor loved but not his family. The movie starts off with a tale about the great great grandfather of Miguel's, a beloved music maker who left his family & never returned (he never returned because he was crushed by that bell) his family saw him as a traitor, putting them off music & would have their jobs in shoe making. Miguel knew how beloved he was, wanting to be just like him while his living fanily disagrees. His adventure was exciting: a living boy going down to explore all his deceased relatives in person & try to find the people who experienced their final death. Nice plot twist during so when it turns out Hector was his true great great grandfather & what they thought his "great great grandfather" was really was nobody to listen to - he poisoned Hector to steal his songs (the villain with jealousy of another's fame). Everybody around Miguel understood & changed their minds. Remember me. And all my reviews.
